ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general milk tea class (FOODON_03301099). No specific Ovaltine milk tea entry exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP covers tea-based beverages with milk as the nearest parent category. No Ovaltine-specific code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept exists for Ovaltine milk tea as a prepared beverage product.

serving.description: 1 large cup (approximately 500 ml / 500 g as served with ice)
serving.natural_language_portion: One large takeaway cup of Ovaltine milk tea as served at KOI Thé Singapore, approximately 500 ml including ice, equivalent to roughly 500 g.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, OVALTINE, powder — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 14355); supplemented with whole milk nutrient data (FDC ID 01077) and standard tea base dilution for prepared milk tea estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct USDA or HPB entry exists for prepared Ovaltine milk tea as served at KOI Thé. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy Ovaltine powder (FDC 14355) combined with whole milk (FDC 01077) and a tea base, proportioned to a typical Taiwanese-style milk tea preparation (approximately 30 g Ovaltine powder, 150 ml whole milk, 320 ml tea/water per 500 ml serving). Per-100g values are derived from this composite. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Foster-Powell K, Holt SHA, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on malted milk drink GI values (GI ~55–60 for Ovaltine-type malted beverages with milk)
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for KOI Thé Ovaltine milk tea. GI of 55 is estimated from peer-reviewed GI tables (Atkinson et al. 2008, Am J Clin Nutr) for malted milk beverages with added sugar and milk. Ovaltine-type malt drinks with milk and sugar have reported GI values in the range 55–60. A value of 55 (low category) is used as a conservative estimate. Glycemic load per 500 g serving calculated as (55 × 60 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 33.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~190 mg per 500 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single large serving of Ovaltine milk tea contributes approximately 190 mg sodium, which is within acceptable limits for most hypertensive individuals, but added sugar and caloric load should be monitored if consumed daily.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content and low-to-medium glycemic index leading to significant glycemic load (~33 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid large Ovaltine milk tea servings due to the high sugar content (approximately 52.5 g sugar per 500 ml serving) and a glycemic load of approximately 33, which substantially elevates postprandial blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from whole milk and high added sugar contributing to cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of large Ovaltine milk tea is associated with moderate cardiovascular risk due to saturated fat (~4.5 g per serving) and high added sugar (~52.5 g per serving), which may adversely affect lipid profiles and contribute to excess caloric intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ovaltine Milk Tea (Large), known in Chinese as 大杯阿华田奶茶 (dà bēi ā huá tián nǎi chá), is a popular Taiwanese-style milk tea beverage served at KOI Thé, a well-known Taiwanese bubble tea chain with outlets across Singapore. The drink is prepared by blending Ovaltine malt powder — a Swiss-origin malted barley and milk powder product fortified with vitamins and minerals — with whole milk, a tea base, sugar syrup, and ice, then shaken or blended to produce a smooth, creamy, chocolatey-malt flavoured cold beverage. The large (L) size is approximately 500 ml. The flavour profile is sweet, malty, and milky with a light cocoa undertone characteristic of Ovaltine. Nutritionally, a large serving provides approximately 340 kcal, 60 g carbohydrate (predominantly from added sugar and malt), 10.5 g protein, and 7.5 g fat. The glycemic load per serving is high (approximately 33), making this beverage a significant source of rapidly absorbed carbohydrates. It is not suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or those on calorie-restricted diets without portion adjustment. The drink contains milk (dairy allergen) and gluten from barley malt. It is vegetarian and generally considered halal. Ovaltine milk tea is a culturally popular comfort drink in Singapore and Taiwan, frequently ordered as an afternoon or post-meal beverage.
common_user_queries: How many calories in KOI Ovaltine milk tea?
Is Ovaltine milk tea suitable for diabetics?
KOI Thé Ovaltine large size calories

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any approved primary source for KOI Thé Ovaltine milk tea as a prepared beverage. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy Ovaltine powder (FDC 14355) and whole milk (FDC 01077) using a proportioned composite model. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed GI tables for malted milk beverages. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from a composite model using USDA SR Legacy Ovaltine powder (FDC 14355) and whole milk (FDC 01077); no direct prepared-beverage entry exists in any approved primary source. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy source for this product formulation. 3. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy Ovaltine powder entry. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy Ovaltine powder entry. 5. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy Ovaltine powder entry. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy Ovaltine powder entry. 7. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy Ovaltine powder entry. 8. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy Ovaltine powder entry. 9. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Am J Clin Nutr) for malted milk beverages; gi_category is low. 10. Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (55 × 60 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 33.0. 11. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Mixed' (input) to 'Beverages' as this is a prepared milk tea drink. 12. Cuisine is retained as Taiwanese per input, consistent with KOI Thé brand origin. 13. Multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean dish multilingual rules applicable to Singapore-served items.

improvement_actions_taken: Reclassified food_group from 'Mixed' to 'Beverages' to accurately reflect prepared milk tea beverage.
Added full multilingual local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singapore-served item multilingual rules.
Constructed composite nutrient estimate from USDA SR Legacy Ovaltine powder (FDC 14355) and whole milk (FDC 01077) with documented methodology.

Assigned GI of 55 (low) as analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed GI tables for malted milk beverages, and corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' to align with the GI value of 55.
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes with explicit source-not-reported justification.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent — all language-specific alias strings appear verbatim in flat aliases list.
Set ontology match_type values exclusively to allowed values: closest_parent and none.
Added zero-macro justification for trans_fat_g = 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy).
Populated health_context with specific clinical guidance for all three required conditions including Type 2 Diabetes with high concern level due to glycemic load.
